title: Other Services Related to Advertising

narrative.executive_view: This is a heterogeneous catch-all, but promotional-products distribution is the best-supported working dominant subsegment: Census product detail explicitly includes coordinating logoed advertising-specialty production and delivery [S2], while ASI reports a $26.6bn promo industry [S3]. A $1–10m EBITDA target can harvest meaningful quoting, artwork and order-administration savings, but it is not a clean software-like roll-up: supplier-side revenue is in the trade total, client books can be portable, and physical/event-service work is not replaced. I therefore view the code as a conditional relationship-protected reseller opportunity, not a broad AI windfall.
narrative.how_ai_changes_work: AI primarily removes information and coordination work: product search, first-pass artwork, mockups, proof routing, quote preparation, store administration, supplier chasing and billing. ASI reports that 50% of extra-large ($5m+) distributors use AI and that creative copy is the most common use among AI users [S4], which supports rapid tool diffusion but not full task replacement at smaller firms. In-person demonstrations, sampling, installation and the physical production and delivery of merchandise remain human or supplier work; salespeople and account leaders still own the brief and relationship.
narrative.value_capture: The economic model is markup resale and managed-program service, not a billable-hour consultancy. 4imprint's 2025 results show $1.347bn revenue, $436m gross profit and $145m operating profit [S5], demonstrating that scaled direct distribution can retain healthy economics. That is a benchmark, not a comp for a small independent: online self-service makes search, artwork and ordering more contestable and is likely to pass a material part of labor savings to buyers. I set capture below one-half on both distribution and moat, because company stores and supplier access help retention but portable sales books and transparent quotes limit it.
narrative.adoption_timing: The reported 50% AI-use rate applies to extra-large distributors, not the full code or the lower-middle market [S4]. I therefore estimate current effective adoption at 35%, with roughly 2.5 years to 50% effective adoption as tools move from copy and mockups into quote-to-cash workflows. This is deliberately slower than casual AI use: integration with supplier data, approval workflows and client systems matters more than access to a general model.
narrative.consolidation_economics: There is real, current consolidation: HALO acquired What's Happening Promotions in October 2025 [S6], BDA acquired Harper + Scott in September 2025 [S7], and Overture acquired Pride Products in January 2026 [S8]. These facts support active buyers, not a claim that every small distributor is buyable or that deal terms are known. The selected 4.0x buy and 5.0x exit multiples sit inside Windsor Drake's Q1 2026 marketing/digital-agency $3m–$10m EBITDA range of 4.0x–6.5x [S9], but that is an adjacent broad category; a target with concentrated or salesperson-owned accounts warrants the low end.
narrative.terminal_demand: Demand for physical branded merchandise is more durable than the distributor's intermediation. Census describes a 541890 product as coordinating production of logoed specialties and delivering them to the client [S2], and AI cannot itself manufacture or distribute the object. The pressure is that AI-native search and self-service can reduce the value of the independent coordination layer. I classify terminal demand as PRESSURED rather than MELTING: enterprise program management, fulfillment and trusted account service can persist, but a bare-bones reseller has a credible bypass risk.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: The code includes merchandise coordination, demonstrations and other advertising services, so no single trade statistic maps cleanly to it. ASI's $26.6bn figure covers a broader promotional-products ecosystem [S3], and its 50% AI statistic is for extra-large distributors [S4]. The deterministic OEWS proxy is 4-digit 5418 and imports agency roles, so the role estimate is sensitive. There is no verified, code-specific owner-age survey or disclosed small-target transaction multiple in the reopened sources; those inputs are deliberately low-confidence estimates rather than invented facts.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS definition: 541890
sources.what_it_supports: Official NAICS context: 541890 is the residual advertising-services industry after named advertising categories are excluded.

sources.title: NAPCS product list for NAICS 54189
sources.what_it_supports: The 541890 product list includes coordinating production and delivery of logoed advertising specialties and merchandise demonstrations.

sources.title: ASI Releases 2025 Counselor State of the Industry
sources.what_it_supports: ASI characterizes the promotional-products industry as $26.6bn and bases its State of the Industry on distributor and supplier surveys.

sources.title: ASI 2025 Counselor State of the Industry release
sources.what_it_supports: The release reports that 50% of extra-large distributors with $5m+ sales use AI and says creative copy is the most common use among AI users.

sources.title: 4imprint Financial Highlights
sources.what_it_supports: A scaled North American promotional-products distributor reported 2025 revenue, gross profit and operating profit, illustrating a viable direct-distribution model.

sources.title: HALO Acquires What's Happening Promotions
sources.what_it_supports: HALO announced its October 2025 acquisition of a promotional marketing agency.

sources.title: BDA acquires Harper + Scott
sources.what_it_supports: BDA announced its September 2025 acquisition of a creative branded-merchandise agency.

sources.title: Overture Promotions acquires Pride Products
sources.what_it_supports: Overture announced its January 2026 acquisition of a promotional-products distributor.

sources.title: Windsor Drake EBITDA Multiples by Industry, 2026
sources.what_it_supports: Its Q1 2026 observations show a 4.0x–6.5x $3m–$10m EBITDA range for marketing/digital agencies and identify retention and specialization as valuation drivers.

ai_replaceable_share.rationale: Automation is concentrated in creative, quoting and order operations; physical demos and relationship selling remain. The supplied 5418 occupation proxy is agency-contaminated, so fractions are bounded judgments, not observed occupation measurements.
caveats: 4-digit 5418 is an agency-heavy proxy rather than a clean 541890 staffing census.
The role mix is reinterpreted toward the promo-products dominant-subsegment hypothesis; demonstration/event firms would have lower automation.

role_judgments.rationale: Search, quote and proof preparation automate; account ownership remains human.
Proxy role's writing and recap tasks automate, though it is not core to the code.
Reporting and program analysis automate, but this is agency-proxy contamination.
Operations reporting automates; supplier negotiation and key accounts do not.
Artwork variants, logo placement and proofs are highly automatable.
Scheduling can automate, while event and demonstration performance is physical.
Proposal administration automates; relationship selling persists.
Visual planning tools help, but physical display execution remains.
Campaign reporting and routine planning automate; commercial judgment remains.
Concept exploration accelerates, while approval and brand judgment remain.
Residual is assumed to include order operations and fulfillment coordination, mixed with physical work.

pi_dist.rationale: Managed programs can retain some workflow savings, but self-service and transparent quoting should pass through a material share.
caveats: 4imprint is a large direct operator, not a lower-middle-market independent.

pi_moat.rationale: Supplier coordination and enterprise program service create some stickiness, but a salesperson-owned book and direct platforms weaken defensibility.
caveats: No source in this packet measures account portability for 541890 targets.

t50_years.rationale: Large distributor use is already material, but effective adoption through source-to-order workflows is below 50% in the inferred target set.
caveats: The observed 50% figure is only for extra-large distributors and measures use, not effective workflow adoption.

current_adoption_pct.rationale: 35% cautiously extrapolates from 50% AI use among $5m+ distributors to a more mixed lower-middle-market target universe.
caveats: Not a directly observed full-industry or NAICS adoption rate.

historical_analogs.value: Online custom-merch and direct-distribution models move search, mockups and ordering toward self-service while leaving physical production and fulfillment in the value chain.
historical_analogs.rationale: The direct distributor model is a relevant prior channel-shift analogue, not evidence of a measured causal effect.
historical_analogs.plausible_range: Partial channel substitution rather than full product substitution
caveats: This is an analogue, not a quantified historical study.

selection.rationale: No reopened code-specific owner-age survey was found; 30% is a conservative planning assumption for a fragmented owner-operated target set.
caveats: Unobserved estimate; do not treat as a demographic fact.

succession_shortage_documented.rationale: No reopened source documents a 541890 succession shortage.
succession_shortage_documented.plausible_range: false unless code-specific evidence is obtained
caveats: Absence of this packet's evidence is not proof of no shortage.

active_consolidators.rationale: HALO, BDA and Overture each announced a recent branded-merchandise/promotional-products acquisition.
active_consolidators.plausible_range: At least 3 visible active buyers; total market count unknown
caveats: Announcements do not show acquisition cadence, target size, or willingness to buy every small distributor.

buy_mult.rationale: Low end of the adjacent $3m–$10m marketing/digital-agency range, reflecting client-book portability and mixed-code risk.
caveats: No disclosed 541890 lower-middle-market transaction multiple was reopened; the cited category is an adjacent proxy.

exit_mult.rationale: A modestly higher exit assumes a de-risked platform with managed programs and retained accounts, still inside the cited range.
caveats: Expansion requires demonstrated retention; recent acquisition announcements disclose no transaction multiples.

pricing_structure.value: Markup resale and managed merchandise-program fees; event/demonstration work is commonly priced per program or event day.
pricing_structure.rationale: Census product detail and the direct-distributor benchmark support a merchandise-coordination model for the dominant-subsegment hypothesis.
pricing_structure.plausible_range: Mixed markup, service-fee and project pricing
caveats: The full catch-all code includes service models that differ from promo distribution.

cross_checks.uplift_vs_A_consistent: A roughly 2.5-year path to 50% effective adoption is consistent with meaningful but staged quote-to-cash automation rather than an immediate labor reset.

terminal_value.justification: Physical merchandise demand survives, but AI-enabled self-service can bypass low-value distributor coordination; enterprise program management is the offsetting channel.

cross_checks.no_proxy_confirmed: No: the deterministic 4-digit 5418 role mix is agency-heavy and the ASI ecosystem includes suppliers outside 541890.
cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation: Transparent online search, mockups and ordering make markup resale vulnerable to pass-through; managed company stores and fulfillment are more defensible.

dataset_mismatch.description: The supplied 4-digit 5418 occupation mix includes agency roles that do not cleanly represent the 541890 catch-all, while the dominant promo-distribution thesis also omits physical fieldwork detail.
sensitivity: A larger event/demonstration mix would lower V.
A more order-operations-heavy distributor mix would raise V.

market_boundary.selection_basis: Census product detail explicitly includes coordinating advertising specialties, and ASI supplies a large promo-industry benchmark; the association total includes suppliers and is not mapped one-for-one to NAICS 541890.

top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: V is highly sensitive to the unobserved promo-versus-event role mix.
M and underwriting returns depend on target-specific customer retention.

reviewer_note: Single-author initial fleet packet. All cited score-driving URLs were reopened on the stated access date; no code-specific owner-age survey or disclosed lower-middle-market transaction multiple was represented as observed.
